Individual Time Trials Little 500 Spring Series
Individual Time Trials Little 500 Spring Series All Day

Bill Armstrong Stadium (Little 500 / Soccer Stadium)

In each heat, one rider will be placed at each of the four corners of the track. From a standing start, at the sound of the horn, each participant will make four complete laps. At the conclusion of the fourth lap, the timers will stop the clock. Riders will be sorted into groups based on their four-...

Rising from the Dust: The Legacy of Michelle Materre exhibit
Rising from the Dust: The Legacy of Michelle Materre exhibit All Day

Black Film Center and Archive, 1320 E. 10th Street, Wells Library 044, Bloomington, IN, 47405 - Room Number: Phyllis Klotman Classroom 044B

Curated by archivists Sarah Petras and Dan Hassoun, this exhibit showcases posters, collages, photos, and realia relating to the career of pioneering film distributor Michelle Materre, including original materials from the first release of Daughters of the Dust! Stop by the BFCA office to get a look...

Black Film Center and Archive, 1320 E. 10th Street, Wells Library 044, Bloomington, IN, 47405 - Room Number: Exhibit/Reception Area

Meet-The-Author: Medinay Bawudun, Rise Up My Uyghurs

Wednesday April 01 - 05:00 PM to 06:00 PM

None

Medinay Bawudun has spent 26 years away from her homeland, yet she continues to stand proudly for Uyghur culture. The Spirit, Shade and Shape of Sasanian Women and Its Impact on Female Images along the Silk Road - a Lecture by Dr. Chen Su
The Spirit, Shade and Shape of Sasanian Women and Its Impact on Female Images along the Silk Road - a Lecture by Dr. Chen Su

Wednesday April 01 - 05:00 PM to 06:00 PM

GA - Room Number: 3067

By synthesizing archaeological remains with textual evidence, this lecture explores the multifaceted roles and images of Sasanian women through the lens of late antique Persian culture. Under the influence of Zoroastrian dualism, the perception of women in ancient Iran was divided into two categorie...

April First Thursdays Festival
April First Thursdays Festival

Thursday April 02 - 04:00 PM to 07:00 PM

ACES Horizon Talk: Dr. Charlotte D'Evelyn, "The Choor Fiddle and Sonic Imagination of the Past in Inner Mongolia, China"
ACES Horizon Talk: Dr. Charlotte D'Evelyn, "The Choor Fiddle and Sonic Imagination of the Past in Inner Mongolia, China"

Friday April 03 - 12:00 PM to 01:00 PM

None

Musical practices of the Mongol people have become increasingly well known around the world, particularly Mongolian throat-singing and the two-string horsehead fiddle, morin khuur. Much less is known about treasured local fiddle forms in Inner Asia, such as the ikel from Western Mongolia and the cho...

Friday April 03 - 12:00 PM to 01:00 PM

GLOBAL & INTERNATIONAL STUDIES BUILDING - Room Number: 1112

Talk abstract: Japanese dialects continue to be used in regions across Japan, despite the promotion of Standard Japanese through education and media. Drawing on examples from Ehime Prefecture, this presentation will discuss how Japanese dialects are evolving and consider what they contribute to the ...

Stavros Halkias: The Dreamboat Tour
Stavros Halkias: The Dreamboat Tour

Thursday April 09 - 07:00 PM to 09:00 PM

Buskirk-Chumley Theater,114 East Kirkwood Avenue, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA

Stavros Halkias is a comedian, actor, and writer from Baltimore, Maryland, now based in New York City. He most recently wrote, produced, and starred in the indie comedy Let’s Start a Cult. Molly Schnell, Assistant Professor, Northwestern University: "Selection into Medicine"
Molly Schnell, Assistant Professor, Northwestern University: "Selection into Medicine"

Thursday April 16 - 09:30 AM to 11:00 AM

SPEA /PUBLIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL AFFAIRS, PAUL H. O'NEILL GRADUATE CENTER - Room Number: A225

Molly Schnell's work centers on health care and industrial organization. Her research focuses on understanding the causes and consequences of provider behavior, specifically in regards to the opioid crisis, and her more recent work has shed light on the economic impact of school shootings.

Thursday April 16 - 12:00 PM to 01:00 PM

GLOBAL & INTERNATIONAL STUDIES BUILDING - Room Number: 4067

This lecture introduces Belarus as a historical crossroads often overlooked in Western narratives. GLOBAL & INTERNATIONAL STUDIES BUILDING - Room Number: 4067
